title: ver: 1.78.2: The application crashes with an error when closing a tab with open connections.
author: Inf-Sub
body_snippet:
```
OS: Microsoft Windows NT 10.0.26100.0
mRemoteNG Connection Manager Version: 1.78.2 (Nightly Build 2932) (and earlier: 1.77.1, 1.77.3)
Edition: MSI
```

```
Cannot access a disposed object.
Object name: 'ConnectionWindow'.
```

```
   at System.Windows.Forms.Control.MarshaledInvoke(Control caller, Delegate method, Object[] args, Boolean synchronous)
   at System.Windows.Forms.Control.Invoke(Delegate method, Object[] args)
--- End of stack trace from previous location ---
   at System.Windows....

comments.author: robertpopa22
comments.snippet:
Likely addressed by close-panel stability package in PR #3106 (same panel-close/disposed connection path). 
 
Please retest on latest fork RC candidate build; if no longer reproducible, this can be closed as duplicate of #3069.

comments.author: robertpopa22
comments.snippet:
This appears to match the same ConnectionWindow.Prot_Event_Closed disposed-object close-path as #3069. Candidate upstream fix package is PR #3106: PR #3106 on `mRemoteNG/mRemoteNG` After merge in parent, please retest on a build that includes that commit; if still reproducible with exact steps/logs, keep this issue open, otherwise close as duplicate of #3069.

---

notes: Manual triage: Duplicate of #1656/#3062/#3069 - disposed ConnectionWindow crash on tab close. Already fixed in v1.79.0 PR #3106 with comprehensive dispose guards.
